creator: wxs@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Thu, May 29, 2008 at 08:10:04AM +0000, Mark Round wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/123704; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: "Mark Round" <mark.round@gmail.com>
> To: bug-followup@freebsd.org
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/123704: sysutils/heartbeat port does not build, patch included
> Date: Thu, 29 May 2008 09:08:31 +0100
> 
>  Config.log is at :
>  
>  http://www.markround.com/static/config.log

OK, I finally managed to reproduce this.  It doesn't reproduce in a
tinderbox, which is odd, but I got it to reproduce on a physical system.
Apparently the Makefile for heartbeat will re-run autoconf right away.

The attached patch is the one I would like to commit for the port.  It
replaces the call to autoconf in Makefile.in with 'true'.  With this
change I was able to properly build heartbeat.

Scott, do you approve of this patch?  I think it's a cleaner solution
since now the port will not run autoconf a second time.

-- WXS
